assets.alt: Three flat glass artifact panels connected in a loop on a blue and mint mesh background
assets.caption: A public content loop starts with real work, turns it into an inspectable artifact, and uses feedback to shape the next version.
assets.generation_method: built-in image_gen, saved post-local as PNG and converted to WebP
assets.notes: Selected variant uses three head-on liquid-glass artifact panels on a restrained sky blue and mint mesh background. The output includes simple loop arrows because the article's core metaphor is a public feedback loop.
assets.final_prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: wide blog hero for Sell In Public blog, final should work as a 1600x700 landscape crop
Source context from article: The article analyzes how Clay, Lovable, and GitLab build public content loops from real work. Clay turns GTM workflows into reusable Claybooks and systems posts. Lovable turns builder activity into launch surfaces, remix paths, docs, and public correction. GitLab turns operating knowledge into a public handbook backed by contribution paths. The central idea is that public authority comes from making real work inspectable, then using feedback or updates to shape the next public artifact.
Primary request: Create one simple visual metaphor inferred from that article context: a public expertise loop made from three inspectable artifacts. Use one or two relevant elements only: three simple flat liquid-glass panels arranged in a loose circular loop around a small central feedback mark. The panels should feel like workflow, launch, and handbook artifacts without using readable text, logos, brand marks, or exact product UI.
Style reference: Sell In Public Flat Liquid Mesh Hero style. Generated raster PNG. Background uses one main color plus at most one close complementary color. Matte translucent liquid-glass shapes. Simple head-on flat UI. Consistent white outline weight. Clean negative space. Subtle grain.
Composition: Wide landscape hero near 1600x700. One centered head-on graphic with quiet space around it. All glass/white graphics must be completely flat and viewed straight-on, like a 2D interface parallel to the image plane.
Scene/backdrop: Soft mesh gradient using only sky blue and mint. No third color family. Blend smoothly without hard edges.
Subject: Three front-facing flat liquid-glass artifact panels in a simple loop around a small abstract feedback mark. Each panel can contain a few short non-readable white lines or simple shapes only. No numbers, no charts, no dashboards, no company logos.
Detail density: Low. Simple, spacious, specific, editorial B2B.
Constraints: Generated raster PNG style only. No readable text, logos, watermarks, exact LinkedIn UI, fake metrics, numbers, charts, line graphs, bar graphs, scattered nodes, random lines, icon clouds, fake dashboards, overcomplicated UIs, generic LinkedIn-post-card default, glossy 3D objects, glow, bloom, flares, light trails, shiny/specular/reflection cues, bokeh/orbs, hard gradient edges, SVG/vector look, photoreal stock-photo look, more than two background colors, angled perspective, isometric view, tilted panels, 3D depth, shadows, or three-quarter UI view.
